L1.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in the request verification endpoint. When a reverse proxy forwards a malformed URI, the URL parsing fails, but the error handling path incorrectly returns a successful authentication response instead of rejecting the request as unauthorized. An attacker who crafts a request with an unparseable URI can bypass authentication entirely, gaining access to protected resources without valid credentials.

ground_truth.reason: When URL parsing fails in the authentication verification handler, the error response incorrectly returns an HTTP 200 (success) instead of HTTP 401 (unauthorized), allowing attackers to bypass authentication with malformed request URIs.
